name: SHARED-CLASS-EMAIL-CARTEL
novelty: Baseline cheap-Sybil: N distinct passports that all bind the SAME anchor CLASS (EMAIL), proving the maximum-independent-set collapse is the load-bearing floor.
construction: Mint 200 ed25519 passports S1..S200. Each binds exactly one EMAIL anchor (independence_weight 0.10, rep_cap 0.30) from a single disposable-domain registrar, so every binding lands in the SAME deterministic per-anchor classId (or, worse, the operator-fleet-cap classId for that registrar/ASN). All 200 are fresh (no ratify history) => reputation reads EXACTLY 0. Sequence: for each Si call writeFact(entity='ACME-CORP', attribute='ceo', value='MALLORY', sourceStamp=Si). One honest source H (DOMAIN anchor, distinct class, reputation earned to 0.45 over 90 days via prior independent ratifications) earlier wrote value='ALICE'. Then call adjudicate('ACME-CORP.ceo'). No time-advance needed.
expected: DEFENDED. independentRootCount over the 200 attacker roots is a clique-less 'independent' graph (all share one anchor class => pairwise NOT independent) so they collapse to a SINGLE witness; the contradiction set therefore has 2 effective independence classes (attacker=1, honest=1). The decisive-or-defer gate sees attacker LCB ~0 (fresh) vs H LCB derived from earned 0.45: gap >= 0.30 and winner >= 0.20 => RESOLVED for ALICE, MALLORY demoted. Headcount of 200 contributes nothing.
mechanism: MIS anchor-disjointness collapse (a) + Beta fresh-source-reads-0 (b) + decisive earned-margin adjudicator (c).
probes: Fully defended. This is the canonical case the engine is built to win; included as the control/regression anchor for the family.

name: BARE-KEY-DRIP-BOMB
novelty: Distinct-key contradiction bomb: 500 DISTINCT bare-key passports (no anchor at all), each asserting a DIFFERENT individually-plausible value, weaponizing denial-of-answer rather than overturning truth.
construction: Mint 500 distinct bare-key passports B1..B500 (passport-only, anchor_set empty => independence_weight 0.00, class = BARE_KEY for all, reputation 0). Each Bi writes a unique plausible value: writeFact('ACME-CORP','hq_city', value=Vi) where Vi are 500 different real cities, one per source, dripped one-at-a-time over simulated weeks (time-advance 1 day between each so none looks like a burst). No incumbent honest value is written first (attacker races to be the only content). Then a late honest source H (DOMAIN, rep 0.5) writes value='BERLIN'. Call adjudicate('ACME-CORP.hq_city') after each drip and again after H.
expected: PARTIALLY DEFENDED / DoS-LEANING. All 500 bare keys share BARE_KEY class => collapse to ONE witness with reputation 0; no single value reaches a decisive earned margin, and pre-H every adjudicate returns DEFERRED (multi-value, all LCB ~0, earned gate fails) => the 500-way dispute piles onto listPending and is NEVER auto-resolved to a false value. Truth is NOT overturned. BUT before H arrives the attribute has no LIVE believed value and the human review queue is flooded with 500 PendingRatification rows — denial-of-answer at linear cost. After H, decisive margin resolves to BERLIN.
mechanism: BARE_KEY class collapse + fresh-rep-0 + defer-not-resolve adjudicator; the residual is queue/answer starvation, not a truth breach.
probes: Documented 'contradiction-set bomb' (the hard theorem's attack 1). The engine prevents a FALSE WIN but does not prevent DENIAL-OF-ANSWER / human-review-queue flooding — a real availability liability worth surfacing.

name: SELF-ECHO-MULTIPLICITY-PUMP
novelty: Single-root echo flood: ONE source re-asserts the SAME (entity,attribute,value) thousands of times to fake corroboration multiplicity and to inflate eviction-protecting independent-source counts.
construction: One source E (single EMAIL anchor, rep 0). E calls writeFact('ACME-CORP','founded_year','1998') 5000 times (identical content_hash each time), optionally interleaving trivial wording variants ('founded in 1998', 'est. 1998') that normalize to the same canonical value. Then E ratifies its own prior strands where the API allows, and lets a true competing value '2001' (from honest H, rep 0.4) sit. Call adjudicate('ACME-CORP.founded_year') and separately drive the forgetting/eviction path on H's '2001' strand to test whether E's inflated count buries it.
expected: DEFENDED. Same-root flood collapses to multiplicity 1 by content_hash dedupe; the corroboration-event ledger records independence-weighted credit only ONCE and same-source agreement confers no independent corroboration. independentRootCount for '1998' stays 1 (single root), so it never reaches the eviction floor's INDEP_SOURCE_COUNT >= 2 nor outranks H. Self-ratify is rejected by the distinct-approver / 'model-is-never-its-own-witness' gate. adjudicate: 1998 LCB ~0 vs H decisive => H wins or single-class echo tiebreak, never majority.
mechanism: content_hash same-root dedupe-to-multiplicity-1 + corroboration ledger exact-once credit + distinct-approver self-ratify rejection + eviction INDEP_SOURCE_COUNT>=1 gate.
probes: Fully defended (echo-collapse invariant). Edge to verify on the real engine: that wording-variant strands truly normalize to one content_hash and cannot each count as a separate provenance root.

name: HEADCOUNT-MAJORITY-FRESH-DISTINCT-ANCHOR
novelty: Genuinely distinct-CLASS headcount: attacker buys 12 cheap-but-DIFFERENT-class anchors (each a real distinct class so MIS does NOT collapse them) and tries to win by sheer corroborated count against one incumbent — no aging.
construction: Acquire 12 sources A1..A12 each with a genuinely distinct realized anchor: mix of separate PHONE numbers (class 0.20), separate cheap DOMAINs (0.35), separate EMAILs on major providers. These are pairwise anchor-disjoint => independentRootCount counts up to ~12 real witnesses (NOT collapsed). All 12 are FRESH (reputation 0). Each writes writeFact('PROJECT-X','status','CANCELLED') and they mutually ratify each other's CANCELLED strands in one burst (no time-advance, so decay hasn't run and α just posted). Honest incumbent H (ORG anchor, rep 0.55, aged) wrote 'ACTIVE'. Call adjudicate('PROJECT-X.status'), then again with { highImpact } context.
expected: DEFENDED. Even with 12 truly-independent classes and mutual ratification, the burst α is independence-weighted but the Beta LCB of each fresh source after a single same-burst ratify is still well below minWinnerReputation 0.20 (one ratify barely moves a 0/0 prior, and LCB prior reads 0). The aggregate is HEADCOUNT, which never drives the gate: adjudicator compares per-source EARNED LCB, sees attacker top LCB ~0.05 < 0.20 and gap < 0.30 => DEFERRED. highImpact additionally demands aged recency-clean + >=2 disjoint anchor classes on the WINNER, which the fresh cartel cannot satisfy => DEFER. CANCELLED never auto-wins; ACTIVE stays LIVE as incumbent until human ratify.
mechanism: Reputation-not-headcount gate (b/c): EARNED LCB margin required, fresh mutual-ratify cannot manufacture earned trust in one burst; highImpact necessary-but-not-sufficient overlay.
probes: Tests the boundary of 'priced not prevented': proves that paying for distinct anchors alone (without TIME/earned reputation) does NOT breach. Sets up attack 5, which adds the missing ingredient.

name: PATIENT-AGED-DOMAIN-CARTEL
novelty: The genuine BREACH: a patient attacker buys K real distinct DOMAIN anchors and spends 90+ days farming EARNED reputation on TRUE claims, then spends that earned trust to land ONE false high-impact value with a decisive margin.
construction: Acquire K=3 genuinely distinct, distinct-class DOMAIN anchors D1,D2,D3 (or 1 DOMAIN + 1 HARDWARE + 1 ORG once those binders exist) — all real, all pairwise anchor-disjoint, none collapsible by MIS. For 120 simulated days: each Di repeatedly writes TRUE, independently-verifiable facts that get ratified by the OTHER two (which, being genuinely independent classes, is legitimate corroboration) plus by ambient honest sources. Drive reputation.ratify on each => each Di's Beta LCB climbs past minWinnerReputation 0.20 toward its 0.60 rep_cap, recency-clean. Time-advance with decay-before-mutate honored so the earned LCB is real, not stale. THEN, on day 120, all three coordinate: writeFact('MERGER-DEAL','acquirer','EVILCORP') (the false value) and mutually ratify it. The honest incumbent H wrote 'GOODCORP' but is lower-rep (0.4) or absent. Call adjudicate('MERGER-DEAL.acquirer', { highImpact: true }).
expected: BREACHED (false value can WIN). Because D1,D2,D3 are GENUINELY independent classes with GENUINELY earned reputation (LCB ~0.45 each, gap over H >= 0.30, >=2 disjoint anchor classes, recency-clean 90-day window all satisfied), the decisive-or-defer gate — and even the stricter highImpact gate — sees a decisive EARNED margin and AUTO-RESOLVES to EVILCORP, demoting GOODCORP. The engine cannot distinguish 3 colluding-but-real witnesses from 3 honest ones; reputation earned on true claims is fungible and spendable on one lie.
mechanism: No structural defense exists; this is the explicit residual. The earned-reputation gate is a POLICY threshold over real anchors, and real anchors are exactly what money buys.
probes: The documented 'priced not prevented' residual (and the hard theorem's core: a patient attacker paying finite cost to mint genuinely-independent sources). This is the intended BREACH — its only mitigations are out-of-band (anchor cost, stake-burn on later disown, human ratify on high-impact) which the spec already concedes.

name: CLASS-COLLISION-LAUNDERING
novelty: Exploits OFFLINE class-assignment error: two attacker sources that are REALLY correlated (same registrar/ASN/operator) but were MISassigned to distinct classIds, laundering one identity into two 'independent' witnesses.
construction: Register 2 DOMAINs D1,D2 through the SAME registrar and on the SAME hosting ASN (truly correlated). Due to an offline class-assignment error (or by deliberately structuring registrations to dodge the operator/fleet-cap classId — e.g. fresh registrar account, rotated WHOIS, different nameserver vendor), the operator assigns D1 and D2 DISTINCT classIds. Both are fresh-ish but earn a modest reputation (LCB ~0.22, just over the 0.20 floor) via a short honest-looking warmup. Then both write writeFact('SUPPLIER-Y','solvency','INSOLVENT') (false) and ratify each other (which the engine now scores as INDEPENDENT corroboration because the classes look disjoint). independentRootCount returns 2. This (1) clears the eviction floor INDEP_SOURCE_COUNT >= 2 so the false strand resists forgetting, and (2) supplies a 2-class dispute. Honest H (rep 0.3) wrote 'SOLVENT'. Call adjudicate('SUPPLIER-Y.solvency'), and run a disown(D1) later to test whether taint-closure catches D2.
expected: BREACHED-CONDITIONALLY. If the misassignment stands, the engine treats one real identity as two independent classes: corroboration credit is conferred mutually (it should have been an echo => 0), independentRootCount=2 lets the false INSOLVENT survive eviction, and the 2-class earned dispute can tip decisive if the laundered pair outranks H. The disown(D1) taint-closure only craters D2 if provenance records them as the same sourceId/class — which by construction it does NOT — so D2's laundered credit survives the sweep. Every downstream bound silently weakens.
mechanism: There is no in-graph defense: independence CLASS ids are offline-assigned and the code trusts them; a class-assignment error is invisible to MIS, to the corroboration ledger, and to the disown taint set (which keys on recorded class).
probes: The documented 'offline class-assignment liability'. Demonstrates it is not merely a weakened-bound nuisance but a corroboration-forgery and eviction-bypass primitive — the single human-judgment input the engine cannot self-check, and the most operationally likely real-world breach.
